title: US launches airstrikes against Iran and Trump rules out prolonged war
summary: US forces carried out new bombings in Iran, while President Trump stated that the offensive would be short-lived and that he does not seek an extensive conflict; Iran threatens retaliation.

title: Reform of the Imperial Household Law advances in the Diet with multipartisan support
summary: The amendment bill that allows succession to the throne in case of emergency passed the debate in the Lower House and is expected to be approved, although criticism persists for gender discrimination.

title: Police and three tech companies agree to share information to prevent credit card fraud
summary: The National Police Agency signed an agreement with Mercari, LINE Yahoo, and Rakuten to exchange data on suspicious transactions and reduce online scams.

title: Japan's long-term interest rates rise to highest level in 30 years, exceeding 2.9%
summary: The yield on the 10-year Japanese bond reached 2.895% due to geopolitical tensions in the Middle East and expectations of monetary tightening.

title: Bankruptcy of payment processor Zenshin leaves thousands of restaurants without service and generates a debt of 600 billion yen
summary: The credit card payment company filed for bankruptcy after years of alleged accounting window dressing, forcing the government to support affected businesses.

title: World Cup advances with victories of Argentina, Switzerland and France in the quarter-finals
summary: Argentina came back against Egypt, Switzerland eliminated Colombia on penalties, and France prepares to face Morocco in the tournament's quarter-finals.

title: Financial markets fall on fears of war escalation between the United States and Iran
summary: Oil rose to $76, the New York Stock Exchange lost 576 points, and the yen weakened amid risk aversion due to the crisis in the Middle East.

title: Germany tightens its immigration policy, Le Pen announces candidacy and a Democrat withdraws in Maine
summary: On the international stage, Germany turns right on immigration, the French far-right runs for 2027, and the Democratic Party faces internal divisions in the United States.

title: Studies reveal that lack of tactile stimulation accelerates aging and that carbonated water relieves brain fatigue
summary: Research at the ISS and the University of Tsukuba shows surprising effects, and a study indicates that 37% of Japanese women experience infertility.

title: Okamoto hits a grand slam and Ohtani reaches 300 home runs in Japanese baseball day
summary: Kazuma Okamoto hit a bases-loaded home run, Shohei Ohtani reached 300 career home runs, and the Giants pitcher was defeated by the Tigers' offense.

title: NATO summit concludes with calls to increase defense spending and Japan reaffirms its cooperation
summary: President Trump softened his critical stance toward NATO, while South Korea and Japan showed solidarity with the alliance on security.

title: Saury price reaches historic record of 44,000 yen per kilogram in Kushiro
summary: The first auction of the season recorded the highest price ever seen for this fish due to scarcity and high demand.

title: Japanese government postpones stricter regulation of heated tobacco products due to lack of evidence
summary: The Ministry of Health will maintain current measures as it cannot determine the impact of secondhand smoke from these products.

title: Munetaka Murakami returns to Major League Baseball on June 10 after his recovery
summary: The Japanese slugger completed his adjustment in the minors and will be activated for Monday's game.

title: Roki Sasaki allows three runs but the Dodgers win in his start
summary: The Japanese pitcher could not get the victory, while Shohei Ohtani went hitless in the game.

title: Prosecutors appeal 30-year sentence for Kawamura for homicide in group attack in Hokkaido
summary: Prosecutors consider the sentence imposed on the accused for the death of a person in a collective assault insufficient.

title: Former Tokyo prosecutor faces disciplinary measures for inappropriate relationship with a person under investigation
summary: The prosecutor from the Special Investigation Department allegedly had a romantic relationship with a suspect, which could lead to his dismissal.

title: Calbee resumes color printing on potato chip packages after improving ink supply
summary: The company restored color on the front side of the bags, although the back will remain in two tones.

title: Pressure increases on China in the South China Sea over artificial island construction
summary: The Philippines and other countries seek a coordinated response to Chinese activities that generate geopolitical tensions.

title: Toyama Airport renamed 'Toyama-Takayama Sushi Airport' to attract foreign tourists
summary: The non-binding official designation seeks to promote local cuisine and improve international connectivity.
